etymology.description: Depicts a tiger: 虍 represents the tiger's head; body stylized over time to 儿
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted a complete tiger, with 虍 representing its head, and the body stylized into the shape of 儿 over time. This visual representation of a tiger captures its powerful presence, leading directly to the meaning 'tiger'.

examples.chinese: 老虎是丛林之王(老虎是叢林之王)。
examples.english: The tiger is the king of the jungle.

examples.chinese: 这只老虎很危险(這只老虎很危險)。
在动物园里(在動物園裡)，小孩们喜欢看老虎(小孩們喜歡看老虎)。
examples.pinyin: Zài dòngwùyuán lǐ, xiǎohái men xǐhuān kàn lǎohǔ.
examples.english: In the zoo, children like to watch the tigers.
